channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Trump signs agreement with Iran. And, the president's approval hits record lows
item.description: Trump signed a preliminary agreement with Iran yesterday to end the war and open the Strait of Hormuz. And, the president's approval rating has hit a record low, according to a new NPR poll.
item.content:encoded:
Trump signed a preliminary agreement with Iran yesterday to end the war and open the Strait of Hormuz. And, the president's approval rating has hit a record low, according to a new NPR poll.

(Image credit: Anna Moneymaker)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: The Obama Presidential Center will be dedicated Thursday. Here's what to expect
item.description: The Obama Presidential Center's grand opening ceremony will be a star-studded event. The center's museum highlights the legacy of the former president, but it is not a traditional presidential library.
item.content:encoded:
The Obama Presidential Center's grand opening ceremony will be a star-studded event. The center's museum highlights the legacy of the former president, but it is not a traditional presidential library.

(Image credit: Pablo Martinez Monsivais)
item.dc:creator: Maham Javaid

item.title: As America turns 250, one museum makes history possible to touch
item.description: Federal law requires most museums and other buildings to be accessible to people with disabilities. But access to what's actually inside is often still limited.
item.content:encoded:
Federal law requires most museums and other buildings to be accessible to people with disabilities. But access to what's actually inside is often still limited.

(Image credit: Rachel Wisniewski for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Jonaki Mehta

item.title: What — and who — will be at the Great American State Fair? Here's a primer
item.description: Several state governments have declined to participate in the 16-day event, though organizers say all U.S. states and territories will be represented by booths on the National Mall.
item.content:encoded:
Several state governments have declined to participate in the 16-day event, though organizers say all U.S. states and territories will be represented by booths on the National Mall.

(Image credit: Win McNamee)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: The price of jet fuel is falling, but don't expect airfares to follow any time soon
item.description: The average price of jet fuel has fallen to its lowest level since the beginning of the war with Iran. But aviation experts say the cost of airfare is likely to stay high, at least for now.
item.content:encoded:
The average price of jet fuel has fallen to its lowest level since the beginning of the war with Iran. But aviation experts say the cost of airfare is likely to stay high, at least for now.

(Image credit: Brandon Bell)
item.dc:creator: Joel Rose

item.title: Poll: Most Americans have the summer blues about Trump and the economy
item.description: A new NPR/PBS News/Marist poll finds a record low share of Americans approve of President Trump's job performance and his handling of the economy heading into the summer before a key midterm election.
item.content:encoded:
A new NPR/PBS News/Marist poll finds a record low share of Americans approve of President Trump's job performance and his handling of the economy heading into the summer before a key midterm election.

(Image credit: Charlie Neibergall)
item.dc:creator: Stephen Fowler

item.title: Hegseth announces in Brussels a review of U.S. forces in Europe, and a 'NATO 3.0'
item.description: U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth lashed out at NATO allies on Thursday, announcing a review of American forces in Europe, and calling for a reboot of the organization to turn it into a "NATO 3.0."
item.content:encoded:
U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth lashed out at NATO allies on Thursday, announcing a review of American forces in Europe, and calling for a reboot of the organization to turn it into a "NATO 3.0."

(Image credit: Virginia Mayo)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: All detainees from immigration facility 'Alligator Alcatraz' have been transferred
item.description: All detainees at the detention center in the Florida Everglades, known as "Alligator Alcatraz," have been transferred to other facilities, the Department of Homeland Security said, citing concerns related to the hurricane season.
item.content:encoded:
All detainees at the detention center in the Florida Everglades, known as "Alligator Alcatraz," have been transferred to other facilities, the Department of Homeland Security said, citing concerns related to the hurricane season.

(Image credit: Rebecca Blackwell)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: New Yorkers are set to fete the Knicks with a ticker-tape parade
item.description: New York is celebrating the Knicks in classic style Thursday, throwing a ticker-tape parade for the team that brought home the NBA championship longed for by generations of fans.
item.content:encoded:
New York is celebrating the Knicks in classic style Thursday, throwing a ticker-tape parade for the team that brought home the NBA championship longed for by generations of fans.

(Image credit: Darren Abate)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: FIFA hydration breaks have sparked criticism. But what do they actually do?
item.description: For the first time in World Cup history, FIFA is mandating all soccer players take hydration breaks to protect them from the threats of extreme heat. But the new rule has sparked criticism.
item.content:encoded:
For the first time in World Cup history, FIFA is mandating all soccer players take hydration breaks to protect them from the threats of extreme heat. But the new rule has sparked criticism.

(Image credit: Julio Cortez)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
